pages.raw_text:
Porters local union 1852 held
their installation of officers at the
Cook hall on Howard street last
Tuesday evening. The local porters
are working under charter from
the American Federation of Labor
and have been asked by that organ
ization to assist in organizing an
international porters' union. The
porters are giving the matter due
consideration and may decide to
accede to the request of the A. F.
of L., and it Is not improbable that
in the near future Spokane will be
the headquarters of the porters'
international union.


The following officers were in
stalled: j. c. Lawler, president;
VIM. Lynch, vice president; F.
Smith, secretary; George Parker,
treasurer; H. Colby, guide; c. Mar
tin, sergeant at arms. The reports
of officers showed the union to be
in good shape financially.
